codeReview.summary: Sentry integration is well-implemented following the research doc patterns. All required files exist with proper conditional initialization guarded by DSN presence. Graceful degradation confirmed (build succeeds without SENTRY_DSN). Environment validation uses empty-string-to-undefined coercion. Worker correctly used instrumentation-client.ts (v10+ pattern) instead of the deprecated sentry.client.config.ts mentioned in the feature description. Minor non-blocking issues found.

issues.description: Missing `reset` prop from Next.js global-error signature. Next.js provides both `error` and `reset` props to global-error.tsx. The `reset` prop is a function to retry rendering the segment. Without it, users have no recovery path except a full page refresh. Consider adding: `reset: () => void` to the props and a retry button in the UI.

`sendDefaultPii: true` sends user IP addresses and request headers to Sentry by default. This is fine for most apps but should be a conscious decision. For GDPR-sensitive deployments, this may need to be toggled off. Not blocking for a template, but worth noting.

`tunnelRoute: '/monitoring'` is configured. If a middleware.ts is added later, it must exclude '/monitoring' from its matcher to avoid interfering with the Sentry tunnel. The research doc notes this explicitly. No middleware exists yet, so not blocking, but this should be documented as a convention.

codeReviewIssues.description: Missing `reset` prop from Next.js global-error signature. Users have no recovery path except a full page refresh. Consider adding `reset: () => void` to props and a retry button.
`sendDefaultPii: true` sends user IP and request headers to Sentry. Fine for most apps but should be a conscious decision for GDPR-sensitive deployments.
`tunnelRoute: '/monitoring'` requires any future middleware.ts to exclude '/monitoring' from its matcher. No middleware exists yet, but this should be documented as a convention.

sharedStateObservations.description: Sentry tunnel route '/monitoring' must be excluded from any future middleware matcher. This convention should be documented in AGENTS.md under React/Next.js conventions to prevent future workers from accidentally blocking Sentry event forwarding.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: apps/web/next.config.ts:46 — tunnelRoute: '/monitoring'. Research doc (.factory/research/sentry-nextjs.md) explicitly warns: 'If using tunnelRoute and Next.js middleware, exclude the tunnel route' with matcher example.

sharedStateObservations.description: environment.md was already updated with Sentry env vars (SENTRY_DSN, NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN, SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN). No gap found — this was handled well.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/environment.md contains '## Monitoring' section with all three Sentry env vars documented.

summary: PASS. The Sentry integration is complete and correct. All 7 expected behaviors are met: @sentry/nextjs installed via catalog, config files for client/server/edge exist, next.config.ts wrapped with withSentryConfig, env validation includes optional SENTRY_DSN/NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN with empty-string coercion, instrumentation.ts registers Sentry, global-error.tsx captures exceptions, and graceful degradation works (build passes without DSN). Three non-blocking issues found: missing reset prop in global-error.tsx, sendDefaultPii enabled by default, and undocumented tunnel route middleware exclusion convention.
